title: Circular union with discriminator
description: Self-referential union UnionC = Date | number | string | {a?: UnionC; b?: string} | UnionC[], recursing through the optional object prop `a` and the array arm. Each node resolves by kind; the value-first form uses RT.circular(self => …) so encode/decode follow the recursion to arbitrary depth. Date members encode Date↔ISO.
notes: Recursive union — encoder and decoder must walk the self-reference (nested objects and arrays) without diverging; member selection happens fresh at every level.
pureType:
type UnionC = Date | number | string | {a?: UnionC; b?: string} | UnionC[];
return createJsonEncoder<UnionC>(undefined, {strategy: 'clone'});
